herdrupdate_instructions: Updating restarts the herdr server and ends live agent panes. Shepherd keeps running, no page reload.
herdrupdate_warning: This ends {count} running session(s). Consider finishing or archiving them first.
herdrupdate_busy: Updating… the herdr server is restarting and live panes will end. Shepherd stays up.
herdrupdate_done_ok: Updated to {latest}. Live panes ended; Shepherd stayed up.
herdrupdate_done_fail: Update did not complete (still on {current}). Check the log; you can retry.
emptyherd_lede: Shepherd runs a herd of live Claude Code sessions. Spawn a task and it gets its own worktree, branch, and terminal you steer in place. Discipline is built in: plans face the Plan gate, PRs face the Critic, and the Merge train lands only what is green and current.
emptyherd_verb_decommission_desc: Stops the agent and removes its worktree. Use when a task is done or abandoned.
emptyherd_verb_archive_desc: Files the session away without tearing down work in progress.
emptyherd_verb_ready_desc: Sessions with a green PR park in their own group, waiting on your merge.
emptyherd_nudge_tail: under Settings → Saved steers to quick-launch tasks straight from the backlog.
error_preview_readonly: Preview is read-only. Open Shepherd directly (not through the live preview) to make changes.
controlkey_ctrl_u: Ctrl U, clear line to start
composebar_input_aria: Compose a message: type or dictate, then send
automation_pill_aria: Repo automation: {count} of {total} on. Open settings.
automation_pill_reviewing_aria: A review is in progress. Open repo automation settings.
automation_panel_subtitle: Repo-wide defaults, not this task alone
automation_roles_hint: Saving commits & pushes .shepherd/roles.json to the default branch.
automation_critic_desc: Auto code-review when CI goes green
automation_autoaddress_desc: Feed findings back to the agent automatically
automation_learnings_desc: Inject house rules into new agents
automation_autopilot_desc: Auto-proceed through stops toward a PR
automation_automerge_desc: Land ready PRs automatically (rebases & re-verifies first).
automation_buildqueue_desc: Let the agent author an ordered, self-revising build plan you curate before it runs.
automation_plan_gate_desc: Grill + adversarial plan review before the agent runs
repo_filter_apply_aria: Filter the herd to {repo} agents only
repo_filter_active_aria: Showing {repo} agents only; click to show all repos
automation_critic_detail: With the Critic on, Shepherd watches every pull request an agent opens. The moment its CI checks all pass (turn green), a separate, read-only review agent starts up in an isolated copy of the repo. It reads the pull request's full diff, hunts for bugs, security problems and anything that doesn't actually satisfy the task, and writes back a verdict: either "looks good" or a list of concrete findings, each tied to a file and line. The review never edits your code; it only reports. A diff that was merely rebased and force-pushed is recognised as identical and is not reviewed twice.
automation_autoaddress_detail: Auto-Address closes the loop after the Critic. Whenever the Critic returns findings, the agent that wrote the pull request is automatically handed a tidy list of them and asked to fix them, so you don't relay anything by hand. The agent pushes a fix, CI runs again, the Critic re-reviews the new version, and the cycle repeats until the findings list comes back empty. To stop it running forever, the loop is capped: three rounds by default, adjustable in Settings. If the agent already explained on the PR why it declined a point, that note is passed to the Critic on the next pass so the same objection isn't raised blindly again. This only works while the Critic itself is on.
automation_plan_gate_detail: The Plan gate inserts a checkpoint before any code is written. A gated agent is told to first lay out its plan in a file instead of coding. Once the plan is ready, a separate reviewer agent reads it, pulls in context from the codebase (read-only) and actively tries to poke holes in it, surfacing risky assumptions, missing steps or a wrong approach. Its objections go back to the planning agent, which revises the plan; this repeats until the plan holds up, up to a configurable round limit (five by default, adjustable in Settings). Only then is the agent released to implement. If you're watching live you can also approve early or demand changes yourself. While the gate is active during planning, Autopilot is paused, and it resumes once the plan is approved.
automation_learnings_detail: Learnings lets the team get smarter over time instead of repeating the same mistakes. In the background Shepherd watches sessions for signals (agents getting blocked, stalling or hitting errors) and periodically distills them into proposed "house rules": short pieces of standing guidance, each with a reason and the evidence it came from. Nothing is applied automatically: you review the proposals and promote the ones worth keeping. Every rule you promote is then prepended to the system prompt of every new agent in that repo, so they all start with the lessons already learned. There's a size budget, and the most recently-relevant rules are kept first when space runs short.
automation_autopilot_detail: Autopilot keeps a single agent moving when it would otherwise stop and wait for you. Every time the agent pauses (at a menu, a yes/no question, a stall or "waiting for input") a small classifier reads the terminal to work out why it stopped, then nudges it onward with the right answer: resume, fix a red CI, or open a pull request once the work is done. It only handles these routine stops; anything that genuinely needs a human is escalated to you instead. A step limit (10 by default) stops a runaway loop, and Autopilot normally hands back once a PR is open. It's off during the Plan gate's planning phase and switches itself back on afterwards.
automation_autodrain_detail: Auto-Drain works through your issue backlog on its own. Shepherd watches your tracker for issues carrying a chosen label and, whenever there's spare capacity, automatically spawns an agent to tackle the next one, so a queue of labelled tickets gets drained without you starting each by hand. It respects the rails you set below: how many auto-agents may run at once (Limit), which label to pick up (Label), and a usage ceiling (%) that holds back new spawns when your account is already busy. Each issue it starts is tagged so two Shepherds never grab the same one. When an agent's PR is ready, it's set aside for a human to merge (or handed to Full-auto merge), freeing the slot for the next issue.
automation_automerge_detail: Full-auto merge lands finished pull requests for you, end to end. It only acts on fully-automatic sessions (ones where both Autopilot and this switch are on) and watches each of their PRs. When a PR is genuinely ready (open, CI green, no conflicts, up to date with the main branch, and, if the Critic is on, with a clean verdict on the current version), the Merge train merges it. If a PR has fallen behind the main branch, it first steers the agent to rebase and re-checks everything before merging. Persistent conflicts are capped (5 rebase attempts) so it pauses for you instead of thrashing. Nothing is merged until every one of these checks passes.
automation_buildqueue_detail: The Build queue turns a big task into a reviewable plan before the agent charges ahead. Right after reading the task, the agent writes out an ordered list of steps, which appear here as cards you can edit, reorder or remove. Nothing runs until the plan is approved (you click Approve, or, if Autopilot is on, it's approved for you) after which the agent works through the steps in order, marking each done as it goes, all within one continuous session so no context is lost. The agent can also revise the remaining steps mid-run if it learns something that changes the plan, while completed steps stay put. It's independent of Autopilot and the Plan gate and can be combined with either.
automation_draftmode_desc: Open PRs as drafts; held out of the merge queue until signed off (human approval and/or Critic per the selector below).
automation_draftmode_excludes_automerge: Draft mode and Full-auto merge are mutually exclusive
automation_signoff_needs_critic: Critic / either need the Critic turned on; only human sign-off is available.
feat_critic_body: Critic inspects each pull request's diff and requests changes or leaves comments, like a tireless reviewer on every PR.
feat_auto_address_body: Critic findings are fed straight back to the agent, which fixes them and pushes until the Critic is clear or the round cap is hit.
feat_learnings_body: Distilled house rules from prior agent activity are injected into every new agent session, so your team's conventions propagate automatically.
feat_halt_body: A single red emergency stop in the gear menu brings the whole herd to a halt: confirm it and every running agent's current turn is interrupted at once. Your always-reachable brake for when the herd needs to stand down.
feature_capture_extension_title: Capture a page into a task
feature_capture_extension_body: Install the Shepherd Capture browser extension to turn the tab you're looking at into a task, screenshot and page context attached, in one click.
feat_merge_train_body: The Ready-to-merge group now has a Merge train link that spawns a session to work through the PRs you've parked there, propose a safe merge order, and merge on your approval.
feat_review_cycles_body: The Critic (PR reviews) and the Plan gate now have independent cycle caps you can tune separately in Settings → Session.
feat_merge_in_progress_body: When you start a merge train, the PRs it's working through move into a Merging group with an amber badge instead of staying in Ready to merge, and each clears as it lands.
feat_autopilot_complete_body: Research, issue-creation, and one-off answers now end in a clean Complete state instead of a misleading "paused for input"; Autopilot only pauses loudly for a real decision.
gitrail_send_review_reviewing_title: Critic is re-reviewing; these findings are being re-validated
gitrail_ready_on_title: Marked ready to merge; click to unmark
gitrail_ready_off_title: Mark ready to merge (no further action needed)
gitrail_merge_failed: Merge failed: {reason}. Check the PR, then retry.
gitrail_merge_failed_checks: Merge blocked: CI is red. Fix checks on the PR, then retry.
gitrail_merge_failed_unmergeable: Merge blocked: branch not mergeable (conflict or behind base). Resolve on the PR, then retry.
gitrail_merge_blocked_busy: Merge unavailable: an operation is in progress.
gitrail_merge_blocked_conflict: Merge blocked: branch has conflicts — resolve on the PR first.
gitrail_merge_blocked_protected: Merge blocked: required checks or reviews aren't satisfied — see the PR.
gitrail_merge_blocked_checks: Merge unavailable: a CI check is failing.
gitrail_merge_blocked_draft: Merge unavailable: this PR is still a draft.
gitrail_merge_blocked_behind: Merge blocked: branch is behind base — update it on the PR first.
gitrail_open_pr_failed: Open PR failed: {reason}. Check git host auth (gh login / token), then retry.
gitrail_redeploy_failed: Redeploy failed: {reason}. Retry once the cause is clear.
herd_all_title: Show every session in the herd
herd_ready_title: Show only sessions awaiting you: idle, blocked, or done
herd_merge_train_title: Start a new session that works through the ready-to-merge PRs and suggests a merge order
herd_merge_train_prompt:
Run a merge train for the pull requests I've flagged ready to merge. Review each for correctness and product-fit, propose a safe merge order, and stop for my approval before merging anything; then re-check the queue after every merge, because main moves under you.

Ready-to-merge PRs:
{prs}

If this repo provides a /merge-train command or skill, use it. Otherwise work the train yourself:
1. For each PR, gather status with gh (CI rollup, mergeability/conflicts, how far behind main, and which files overlap other PRs in this list).
2. Move any PR that is CI-red, conflicting, gate-failing, or that we don't actually want into a separate hold list with a one-line reason; never merge past a red gate.
3. Order the rest: smallest and most independent first; when two overlap, sequence them so the riskier one rebases onto the other; release-please PRs last.
4. Present the numbered merge order plus the hold list and WAIT for my approval.
5. On approval, merge in order: rebase onto latest main first (never merge main into the branch), confirm gates are green, then gh pr merge --squash --delete-branch, and re-check the remaining PRs against main before the next one.
issuespanel_no_host: no git host configured for this repo
issuespanel_active_label_title: Claimed by an active Shepherd agent; this issue is already being worked on
newtask_upload_failed: Image upload failed: {reason}. Retry the attach.
newtask_create_failed: Create failed: {reason}. Check repo and base branch, then retry.
newtask_relaunch_note: The original task will be archived; its prompt, model, base branch and attached images carry over.
newtask_relaunch_issue_drop_note: Linked issue #{number} will be unlinked and returned to its backlog.
newtask_relaunch_image_note: This task's images are carried over and kept first; newly attached images past the limit are dropped.
reposelect_recent_agents_one: {count} agent run here in the last {days} days
reposelect_recent_agents_other: {count} agents run here in the last {days} days
clonerepo_failed_auth: Access denied: no credentials for this repo
settings_push_unsupported: Not supported on this browser. On iOS, install Shepherd to your home screen first.
settings_push_denied: Blocked in browser settings. Re-enable notifications for this site, then reload.
settings_about_blurb: Shepherd is self-hosted mission control for interactive Claude Code: genuine terminal sessions you observe and steer by typing, on your own server and subscription. It is equally opinionated about how agent-built software ships: the Plan gate and the Critic review every plan and PR adversarially, approved Learnings become house rules, and the Merge train lands nothing that is red or behind its base.
settings_remote_control_hint: Auto-start Claude Code remote control in agent sessions. Off keeps sessions quiet; turn it on per-session in the terminal with /remote-control.
settings_housekeeping_hint: Daily cleanup that deletes archived sessions older than {days} days or beyond the newest {count}. Off keeps every archived session forever.
settings_pr_review_cycles_hint: How many times the critic re-reviews and steers an agent to address findings on a PR before escalating to you. Higher means more autonomous back-and-forth; lower hands off to you sooner. {min}–{max}, applies to every repo.
settings_pr_review_cycles_save_failed: Couldn't change PR review cycles. Retry.
settings_plan_review_cycles_hint: How many times the plan gate re-reviews and steers an agent to revise its plan before escalating to you. Higher means more autonomous back-and-forth; lower hands off to you sooner. {min}–{max}, applies to every repo.
settings_plan_review_cycles_save_failed: Couldn't change plan review cycles. Retry.
todopanel_empty: No TODO.md yet. Add the first item
star_prompt_body: You've been herding with Shepherd for a few days. If it's been useful, a star on GitHub helps others find it.
star_prompt_error: Couldn't star the repo. Check that gh is signed in, then try again.
feat_star_body: After a few days of use, Shepherd gently asks whether to star its repo with your gh account: one click, or snooze/dismiss. No nagging.
triage_empty: No agents are waiting on you.
triage_stall_note: Quiet. No activity for a while. May be stuck; nudge it or open the terminal.
learnings_badge_tip: What Shepherd learns from your agent sessions: proposed house rules from your corrections, review findings, blocks, and stalls; for this repo only, not global.
learnings_about_lead: Learnings are house rules Shepherd distils from your agent sessions: from your corrections, code-review findings, blocks, and stalls.
learnings_about_scope: They apply per repository, not globally.
learnings_about_flow: A distiller proposes recurring patterns as rules. What you approve is fed into every new agent session for this repo; Promote sends a rule into CLAUDE.md as a pull request.
learnings_open_curate_aria: Open learnings to curate injected rules ({count} over budget)
learnings_empty: No proposed rules yet. The distiller surfaces recurring patterns from agent activity.
learnings_evidence_help: Signals are captured from agent sessions: your corrections, code-review findings, and sessions that blocked or stalled. The distiller proposed this rule from them; they don't come from a PR.
learnings_sources_toggle_aria: Show the signals this rule was distilled from
learnings_overbudget_title: Over the injection budget, so not prepended to new agents. Dismiss an active rule to free up room.
learnings_ineffective_title: A new mistake matched this rule; the distiller flagged it as not working. Reword, strengthen, or dismiss it.
learnings_promote_aria: Promote this rule into CLAUDE.md via a pull request
learnings_promote_started: Opened a CLAUDE.md PR for the rule
learnings_promote_failed: Could not promote the rule; see the server log
viewport_next_needs_you_aria: Jump to the next session waiting for your reply
viewport_usage_title: {input} in · {output} out · {cacheRead} cache read · {cacheWrite} cache write
viewport_meta_aria: Profile and token usage for this task
viewport_preview_setup_hint: Blank? Ensure the preview port is reachable over Tailscale; see setup.
viewport_preview_start_note: Types a dev-server start command into the agent's live terminal
viewport_preview_start_failed: Couldn't start preview; check terminal for details
viewport_preview_stop_note: Stop the previewed dev server to free its memory
viewport_preview_stop_nothing: No running dev-server process was found to stop; the preview is still attached.
viewport_preview_stop_failed: Couldn't stop the dev server; it may be ignoring the stop signal.
unitrow_preview_badge_degraded: Preview; Tailscale registration failed, reachable on loopback only
viewport_preview_serve_failed: Tailscale registration failed for this preview port; it's reachable on loopback only. Use Open in new tab if the frame is blank.
viewport_fold_aria: Collapse header: hide tabs and PR actions for more terminal space
viewport_unfold_aria: Expand header: show tabs and PR actions
criticbadge_title: Critic review for this PR head
criticbadge_stalled_title: Auto-address gave up after {cap} rounds with findings still open; needs a human.
criticbadge_final_title: Addressing the last allowed round; no more rounds after this.
newtask_plan_gate_hint: Grill + adversarial review before the agent runs
planpanel_review_unchanged: Plan unchanged since the last review; nothing to re-check.
planpanel_review_failed: Couldn't start the review. Please try again.
viewport_ctx_repo_toggle_aria: Repo {repo}; tap to show or hide the repo name
viewport_decommission_ready_title: PR is up. Close this session & clean up its worktree
viewport_decommission_ready_aria: PR ready. Decommission unit and clean up worktree
leftover_desc: These were started during this session and are still active. Terminate them too?
clearmerged_desc: These {count} sessions are on merged branches. Clearing them stops each agent, removes its worktree, and deletes the merged branch.
clearmerged_leftovers: {count} background processes from these sessions will also be terminated.
viewport_keynav_hint: j/k/1–9 switch session: hold {key} from terminal
viewport_redraw_title: Redraw: fix squished terminal text (test variants)
redrawmenu_nudge_hint: Briefly shrinks the terminal by one column and back so Claude repaints the visible screen.
redrawmenu_reattach_hint: Reconnects this view to the agent at the current size.
redrawmenu_fullscreen_hint: Sends /tui fullscreen; Claude then manages and re-renders its own scrollback.
redrawmenu_resume_hint: Respawns claude --resume and re-renders the full history at the current width. Aborts a running turn!
relaunch_archive_failed: Relaunched, but couldn't decommission the original; close it manually
relaunch_issue_unresolved: Couldn't re-resolve the linked issue; original kept, retry later
relaunch_failed: Couldn't relaunch; the original was kept
relaunch_images_carry_failed: Couldn't load the original's images — re-attach them if needed.
viewport_reconnect_sub: herdr is down. Tap once it's back.
topbar_clock_tip_connected: Live connection to herdr · device clock
steerbar_broadcast_aria: Broadcast a steer to multiple sessions
steerbar_coach_hint: Tap a chip to steer the agent. Broadcast sends to many at once.
broadcast_failed: Broadcast failed: no steers delivered. Check the targets, then retry.
toast_merge_train_no_prs: No ready-to-merge PRs to run a merge train on
toast_merge_train_other_repos: {count} ready-to-merge PRs in other repos were left out; run a separate merge train there
toast_merge_train_mark_failed: Merge train started, but marking its PRs failed.
toast_update_main_offer: PR merged. Update your local default branch?
toast_update_main_wrong_branch: Local checkout isn't on {branch}; left it untouched
toast_update_main_dirty: Local {branch} has uncommitted changes; left it untouched
updatemodal_status: ⟳ Updating… The server is restarting; the page will reload automatically.
updatemodal_deploy_failed: The update could not be completed. The deploy stopped before the server restarted.
updatemodal_unreachable: The server stayed unreachable for too long. The update may have failed. Reload the app or check the logs.
actionspanel_unavailable: Actions are not available for this repository
prspanel_review_button_title: Open a review task seeded with this PR
prspanel_rebase_button_title: Ask Dependabot to rebase this PR
halt_all_aria: Halt the herd: interrupt all {count} running agents
halt_arm_aria: Confirm halt; activate again to interrupt all {count} running agents
halt_failed: Halt failed: no agents interrupted. Retry.
session_autopilot_paused_title: Autopilot handed this back for a decision: {question}
session_autopilot_complete_title: Autopilot finished: task complete, nothing to open as a PR: {summary}
session_autopilot_toggle_failed: Couldn't change autopilot for this session. Try again.
readiness_load_error: Couldn't load the readiness scorecard. Try again.
readiness_not_applicable_body: The readiness baseline currently covers JavaScript / TypeScript repositories. Support for other stacks is on the way.
readiness_all_covered: Every baseline guardrail is in place.
readiness_claudemd_present_note: This repo already ships agent instructions; the task will merge, not overwrite.
readiness_adopt_intro_create: Make this repository AI-ready: install the guardrails below and add a CLAUDE.md with these house rules. Open a PR; don't commit to the default branch.
readiness_adopt_intro_merge: Make this repository AI-ready: install the guardrails below and merge these house rules into the existing CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md without overwriting it. Open a PR; don't commit to the default branch.
readiness_g_pre_push_ci_removes: Without it the agent pushes red and waits on a human CI round-trip to learn what broke.
readiness_g_git_hooks_removes: Without a hook manager, none of these checks run automatically before a push.
readiness_g_type_checker_removes: Without it, type errors surface in your review instead of failing the agent first.
readiness_g_linter_removes: Without it, you flag lint nits by hand that the gate could catch.
readiness_g_formatter_removes: Without it, you hand-fix style in review; adopt a formatter plus lint-staged.
readiness_g_test_runner_removes: Without tests in the hook, regressions reach you instead of failing the agent first.
readiness_g_agent_instructions_removes: Without house rules, you re-explain the same posture in chat every task.
readiness_g_ci_removes: Without CI, nothing independently re-checks what the agent self-reported.
readiness_g_lint_staged_removes: Without it, the whole tree is re-checked or skipped; staged-only keeps the hook fast.
readiness_g_commit_lint_removes: Without it, you correct commit-message format by hand.
readiness_g_dead_code_audit_removes: Without it, dead exports and unused deps accrete and you spot them by eye.
readiness_adopt_gitignore_note: Opens a PR adding Shepherd's session artifacts to .gitignore for repos you can push to; otherwise they're already hidden locally.
readiness_adopt_gitignore_no_access: No push access; session artifacts are already hidden locally via git exclude.
readiness_adopt_gitignore_no_forge: No git forge configured; session artifacts are already hidden locally via git exclude.
feat_adopt_gitignore_body: The Readiness tab can now open a PR adding Shepherd's session artifacts (.shepherd-*) to a repo's .gitignore in one click, for repos you can push to. Where you can't, they stay hidden locally via git exclude, so they never clutter your diffs either way.
feat_pr_kind_badges_title: Code vs. bot PRs in the backlog
feat_pr_kind_badges_body: The backlog repo list now shows your real code-PR count, with separate badges for Dependabot and release-please PRs so automated PRs don't look like pending work. The PRs tab tags each PR by kind too.
feat_relaunch_body: Right-click (or long-press) any card and choose Relaunch to spawn a fresh task with the same prompt and current settings (model, repo, base branch and plan-gate included) then decommission the original. The replacement starts fresh: with plan-gate on it re-plans, and a task that came from the drain becomes an attended task awaiting your Approve.
feat_title_rename_body: Double-tap (or double-click) the session name in the header to rename it right there — no need to hunt for the little ✎ button. A single tap still shows the model profile and token usage.
feat_relaunch_repo_body: Relaunch a task in another repository — the New Task dialog opens pre-filled with the task's prompt, model and base branch so you can pick a different repo to land it in.
feat_readiness_body: The Backlog page has a new Readiness tab: it scores a repo's guardrails against Shepherd's dogfooded baseline, prescribes the tooling that removes the most human↔AI back-and-forth, and generates a tailored CLAUDE.md you can send straight to a task.
draft_reconcile_promote_error: Draft promote failed for {desig}; check the PR and retry
draft_reconcile_enforce_error: Draft enforce failed for {desig}; check the PR and retry
feat_automerge_body: Turn on Full-auto merge to let Shepherd land ready PRs for you: it rebases onto main and re-verifies before every merge, so parallel tasks stay safe.
buildqueue_empty: No steps yet. The agent will author a queue when it starts.
feat_buildqueue_body: Enable the Build queue flag on a repo to let the agent author an ordered step list before it runs. You curate the steps (edit, reorder, delete, add) then click Approve & run. Under Autopilot the queue is auto-approved.
feat_plan_gate_body: Turn on the Plan gate to align before the night starts: the agent grills you and writes a plan, then a second Claude adversarially reviews it until it holds up; only an approved plan runs. Enable it per task in New Task or per repo in the automation panel.
feat_backlog_repo_filter_body: Narrow the backlog's repo list to repos with open issues and/or open PRs using the new toggle chips.
feat_newtask_repo_first_title: Repo now leads the New Task form
feat_newtask_repo_first_body: The repository you'll launch into now sits at the top of the New Task form and is named on the Create & Run button, so you never start a task against the wrong repo by accident.
prspanel_merge_train_prompt:
Run a merge train for the pull requests I've selected. Review each for correctness and product-fit, propose a safe merge order, and stop for my approval before merging anything; then re-check the queue after every merge, because main moves under you.

Selected PRs:
{prs}

If this repo provides a /merge-train command or skill, use it. Otherwise work the train yourself:
1. For each PR, gather status with gh (CI rollup, mergeability/conflicts, how far behind main, and which files overlap other PRs in this list).
2. Move any PR that is CI-red, conflicting, gate-failing, or that we don't actually want into a separate hold list with a one-line reason; never merge past a red gate.
3. Order the rest: smallest and most independent first; when two overlap, sequence them so the riskier one rebases onto the other; release-please PRs last.
4. Present the numbered merge order plus the hold list and WAIT for my approval.
5. On approval, merge in order: rebase onto latest main first (never merge main into the branch), confirm gates are green, then gh pr merge --squash --delete-branch, and re-check the remaining PRs against main before the next one.
feat_backlog_pr_merge_train_title: Launch a merge train from selected PRs
feat_backlog_pr_merge_train_body: Multi-select open PRs in a repo's backlog PRs tab and launch a merge train scoped to just those PRs.
feat_header_fold_title: Fold the header for more terminal
feat_header_fold_body: On mobile, tap the chevron in the session header to collapse the tabs and PR rail and hand that space to the terminal. Your choice sticks across sessions.
feat_learnings_per_repo_title: Learnings show on the repo chip
feat_learnings_per_repo_body: A repo's pending house rules show as a ✦ on its chip in the herd's repo filter. Filter to that repo and click the ✦ to open the learnings drawer.
feat_update_checkout_body: When you merge a PR from one of the app's merge buttons, a toast offers to fast-forward that repo's local default branch to the freshly merged state; no manual pull needed.
feat_automation_help_body: Every switch in the automation panel now has a clickable "ⓘ" that opens a thorough, plain-language explanation of what that function does, how it works, and exactly when it fires, so anyone, not just the team, can understand the Critic, Plan gate, Autopilot, Auto-Drain and the rest before turning them on.
feat_critic_activity_title: See what the Critic is doing live
feat_critic_activity_body: The Critic runs as its own Claude Code session. Hover the REVIEWING badge to see its latest action live (“$ git diff”, “read review.ts”) instead of just that it's busy.
feat_preview_body: When an agent's dev server is running, a Preview badge appears on its row. Opening it shows the live app in an in-HUD pane, reachable from desktop or phone over Tailscale.
feat_repo_roles_body: Set who reviews and who merges a repo in its automation panel. When the merger is someone else (e.g. a teammate who lands PRs), a green PR reads "Waiting on them" instead of "Your turn".
feat_backlog_active_highlight_title: See at a glance which issues are already claimed
feat_backlog_active_highlight_body: Issues a Shepherd agent has claimed carry a “shepherd:active” label. In the backlog's issue list it now stands out in amber, so you can tell taken work from free work at a glance instead of reading every chip.
feat_repo_filter_body: Click a repo in the herd's repo filter to show only its agents; click it again to show every repo.
feat_resume_session_body: When Claude exits and leaves a session sitting at a bare shell (e.g. after a herdr restart), it no longer strands you. A ↻ Resume button now appears in the session header (and right-clicking, or long-pressing, any session card offers Resume too) to respawn `claude --resume` and drop you straight back into the conversation.
feat_draft_mode_body: Turn on Draft mode to open every new PR as a draft; it stays out of the merge queue until signed off. Choose who may promote it: a human, the Critic, or either. Mutually exclusive with Full-auto merge.
feat_preview_start_title: Start a dev server from the HUD
feat_preview_start_body: When an agent has no live preview yet, a Start control appears on its Viewport. Click it to have the agent launch its dev server (auto-detected from package.json, or type the command yourself) then preview the running app without waiting for the agent to start one on its own.
feat_active_label_newtask_title: Claimed issues stand out in New Task
feat_active_label_newtask_body: When you seed a New Task from a GitHub issue, any issue already claimed by an active Shepherd agent now shows its shepherd:active label highlighted (surfaced first and tinted with the running-session hue) so you can tell at a glance which issues are already being worked on.
feat_issue_search_body: The Issues tab in the Backlog view now has a search field: type to narrow the list by number, title, body, or label instead of scrolling through dozens of issues.
feat_recent_repos_title: Recent repos pinned to the top of the picker
feat_recent_repos_body: The repo picker in New Task now pins the three repos you've run the most agents on over the last 3 days to the top, set apart and ranked by agent count, so the repos you're actively working on are one click away instead of buried in the alphabetical list.
feat_fable_body: Anthropic's most powerful model is now selectable in New Task. Pick "fable" for the hardest, longest-horizon work. Heads-up: it's the premium tier with higher token costs than Opus, so reach for it when the task is worth it.
feat_tally_filter_body: The Busy / Idle / Blocked counts in the top bar are now buttons: click one to show only sessions in that state, in the session list and the grid view. Click it again, hit Herd, or use the chip in the list header to clear the filter.
fable_arrival_body: Anthropic's most powerful model is now available in Shepherd. Pick "fable" in New Task for the most demanding work. It's the premium tier (higher token costs than Opus) so save it for the tasks that earn it.
feat_preview_tailscale_body: Live previews now register themselves over Tailscale automatically as each agent's dev server comes up, and unregister when it stops, so only in-use ports are exposed. No more one-time tailscale serve port setup.
feat_whatsnew_versiondate_title: Each update is now dated and versioned
feat_whatsnew_versiondate_body: Every entry in this What's-New drawer now carries the release version it shipped in and the date that release went out, so you can see at a glance how recent each change is and which version introduced it.
feat_backlog_recent_repos_title: Recent repos pinned in the backlog
feat_backlog_recent_repos_body: The backlog's repo list now starts with the three repos you've recently run the most agents on, ranked by the same criteria as the New Task repo picker's "recently worked on" group.
feat_product_story_body: Shepherd now says what it is: opinionated mission control for interactive Claude Code. The About section in Settings carries the two-pillar story (genuine sessions you steer by typing, and firm opinions on how agent-built software ships) and the README leads with it.
feat_herd_keynav_body: Reach any session without the mouse: j/k (or the arrow keys) cycle through the herd in rail order, 1–9 jump straight to the Nth session, and g hops to the next session that needs you. The keys stay out of the way while you type in the terminal or a form.
feat_preview_stop_title: Stop a preview to reclaim RAM
feat_preview_stop_body: Force-stop a previewed dev server from the preview pane to free its memory. Operators can also enable opt-in idle-stop (SHEPHERD_PREVIEW_IDLE_STOP_MS) to auto-stop idle, unwatched previews.
feat_card_repo_icon_title: Repo emoji replaces the repo line on cards
feat_card_repo_icon_body: When a repo has a configured emoji, session cards now show it right before the session name instead of spending a whole line on the repo name: denser cards, same at-a-glance repo identity. Hover the emoji to see the repo name; click it to show only that repo's sessions, click again to clear the filter. Repos without an emoji keep the familiar repo line.
unitrow_repo_filter_clear_aria: Clear the {repo} filter, show all sessions
feat_viewport_repo_icon_title: Repo emoji replaces the repo name in the phone terminal header
feat_viewport_repo_icon_body: On small screens, when a repo has a configured emoji, the terminal header now shows just the emoji instead of emoji plus repo name, freeing that width for the session title. Tap the emoji to show the repo name again; tap once more to hide it. Repos without an emoji keep showing their name as before.
feat_slim_header_body: The bar above the terminal is more compact: the dev-server start button is now icon-only, autopilot and status show as tight pills and a status glyph. GitHub Issues have moved out of a per-session Issues tab and now live in the Backlog pane instead.
settings_default_model_hint: Sets the model for every new task and for autonomous drain/autopilot spawns. Auto keeps autonomous spawns on Claude's own default; the launch-window default is the Fable promo if active, otherwise Claude's own default. Choosing an explicit model makes all unattended spawns run that tier.
settings_default_model_premium_warning: This model tier is premium. All autonomous drain and autopilot spawns will run at this cost unattended.
feat_default_model_body: Set a global default model for every new task and all autonomous drain/autopilot spawns from Settings → Session. "Auto" keeps the launch-window default; "default" locks in Claude's own model; or pick a specific alias to always run that tier.
feat_herd_keynav_anywhere_title: Session switching from inside the terminal
feat_herd_keynav_anywhere_body: Session switching now works while you're typing in the terminal: Alt (⌥ on Mac) + j/k, g, or 1–9 switches without leaving the keyboard; Shepherd captures these combos, so those key combinations never reach the agent's terminal. With the terminal unfocused, plain j/k/g/1–9 now chain freely (switching no longer pulls focus back), and Enter returns focus to the terminal.
feat_stepper_legend_title: Task bar shows each pipeline stage
feat_stepper_legend_body: Each session card's progress bar now tracks five stages (Planning, Implementing, PR, Review, and Ready) with the PR segment tinted by CI status and the Review segment by the review verdict. Hover the bar on desktop to see a stage legend.
feat_desktop_decom_body: Hover a session row to reveal a ✕, or use the decommission control now always visible in the session header: two clicks (arm, confirm) decommission any session, PR or not. Right-click a row for the full menu.
feat_usage_limit_push_body: Shepherd now sends a push notification when the 5-hour Claude usage window crosses 80%, once per window, so you can wind down or queue work before hitting the cap.
issuespanel_action_aria: Start a session running “{label}” on this issue; skips the New Task dialog
steerseditor_hint: Each entry can show as a chip in the session steer bar and/or as a quick-launch button on backlog issues. An emoji keeps it recognizable when space is tight.
steerseditor_scope_bar_title: Show as a chip in the session steer bar
steerseditor_scope_issues_title: Show as a quick-launch button on backlog issues
feat_issue_actions_body: Define multiple prompts that launch straight on a backlog issue; the old single standard command became the first one. Steers and issue actions now share one editor in Settings, and each entry can carry an emoji so buttons stay recognizable when space runs out.
steerseditor_scope_none_error: every entry must surface somewhere: enable “bar” and/or “issues” on the highlighted row
feat_steer_labels_body: On the cramped mobile steer bar, tap the right-anchored “ABC” key to show every button's text label instead of just its emoji, so you can tell the steers apart when you forget what a glyph means. Tap again to collapse back to icons. The choice is remembered per device.
feat_usage_gauge_detail_body: Hover the 5H / WK usage bars in the top bar and a detailed card now opens (each window with its full name, a wide bar, the exact percentage and when it resets) instead of the old one-line tooltip.
timetip_waiting_merge_burning: {who}! The merge button is gathering dust! {ago} 🔥
feat_time_tooltip_body: Hover a session card to see what its clock is made of: runtime, last activity, and how long an open PR has been waiting on review or merge, with escalating emoji the longer it sits 🔥.
newproject_name_invalid: Use only lowercase letters, digits, dots, hyphens, and underscores; must start with a letter or digit
newproject_idea_placeholder: Describe what you want to build…
newproject_warning_github: Project created locally; the GitHub step failed, publish it from the terminal.
newproject_failed_slug: Invalid project name: use lowercase letters, digits, dots, hyphens, and underscores only
newproject_failed_exists: A folder with that name already exists
newproject_failed_outside: Target is outside the projects folder
newproject_failed_identity: Set your git identity first: git config --global user.name "Your Name" and git config --global user.email "you@example.com"
newproject_failed_gh_auth: Not logged in to GitHub; run gh auth login.
newproject_failed_gh_exists: A GitHub repo with that name already exists.
newproject_failed_remote: Could not create the GitHub repository.
newproject_failed_git: git failed while bootstrapping the project.
newproject_prd_seed_noidea: (no description was provided; infer a reasonable starting scope and ask clarifying questions in the PRD)
feat_new_project_body: Create a fresh git repo (optionally on GitHub) and kick off the first agent run, all without leaving Shepherd.
feat_sidebar_collapse_body: On touch tablets and unfolded foldables, collapse the Herd sidebar to give the terminal more room. Your choice is remembered.
feat_herdr_release_notes_link_body: The herdr update panel now links to the full upstream release history on GitHub, handy when you're jumping across several versions and want every version's notes, not just the latest.
feat_repo_switcher_title: The repo filter is now a chip rail
feat_repo_switcher_body: The repo filter is now a row of chips above the herd. Click a repo to scope every view to it; click it again to show all repos. A repo's pending learnings and pauses show on its chip, with the rest of its drain detail when you filter to it. Filtering now works in the grid view too.
feat_redraw_menu_body: Terminal history squished after viewing a session from a narrower device? The new ↔ Redraw button in the session header offers four repair variants to try, from a gentle resize nudge to a full claude --resume re-render.
feat_backlog_repo_search_body: Type in the new filter input at the top of the backlog repo list to narrow repos by name; combines with the Has issues / Has PRs chips.
feat_steerbar_edit_body: When the steer bar isn't crowded, a pencil button sits at its right edge — tap it to jump straight into the steers editor.
automation_sandbox_profile_hint: Sandboxing is opt-in. Standard and Autonomous confine the agent to its worktree and block ~/.ssh, ~/.aws, sibling repos, and other $HOME dotfiles. The gh and claude auth tokens it needs to work stay readable — and, until egress is contained, still exfiltratable. Autonomous is required for auto/drain sessions, but is NOT yet network-egress-contained.
automation_sandbox_profile_caveats: Sandboxed repos must use an HTTPS git remote (SSH-remote repos can't push), and MCP servers whose dependencies live outside the bound paths may fail to load.
session_sandbox_degraded_title: Requested sandbox unavailable — this agent is running unconfined.
session_sandbox_autonomous_title: Filesystem-confined to the worktree only; the gh/claude auth tokens it needs stay readable. NOT yet network-egress-contained — outbound access is unrestricted, so those tokens remain exfiltratable. Don't over-trust it.
session_sandbox_standard_title: Filesystem/process-confined to this worktree; blocks ~/.ssh, ~/.aws, and sibling repos. The gh/claude auth tokens it needs stay readable — and, with egress uncontained, still exfiltratable.
session_sandbox_unconfined_title: Unattended agent running unconfined — no sandbox applied.
feat_sandbox_body: Each repo can now pick a sandbox profile in its automation panel: Trusted (no sandbox, today's behavior), Standard, or Autonomous. Standard and Autonomous confine the agent to its worktree. Autonomous is required for auto/drain sessions — note it's filesystem-confined only and not yet network-egress-contained. Sessions surface their applied sandbox state as a badge.
newtask_sandbox_hint: Per-task sandbox override. Leave at 'Repo default' to inherit this repo's configured profile. standard/autonomous confine the agent to its worktree and block ~/.ssh, ~/.aws, and other $HOME dotfiles, but the gh/claude auth tokens it needs stay readable (and exfiltratable until egress lands); autonomous is required for auto sessions but is not yet network-egress-contained.
feat_epic_runner_title: Work an ordered epic of issues
feat_epic_runner_body: Point Shepherd at a tracking issue and it works the child issues in dependency order — one PR per issue, advancing as blockers merge.
feat_native_sub_issues_body: Issues with native GitHub sub-issues (no markdown checklist) now show a SUB-ISSUES badge in the backlog, expandable just like epics.
feat_session_epic_badge_body: A session working a tracking issue now shows an EPIC badge with live sub-issue progress — click it to open that epic in the backlog.
epic_badge_open_title: Epic #{number}: {merged}/{total} sub-issues done — open in backlog
epic_badge_open_aria: Open epic #{number} in the backlog ({merged} of {total} sub-issues done)
